Calgary's Central Library is a stunner to rival Seattle's   Seattle’s library is known for its futuristic design and ‘books spiral’ In 1999, Dutch architect Rem Koolhaas and Seattle native Joshua Prince-Ramus were selected to design Seattle’s Central Library. The pair envisioned a pile of floating boxes, hovering at different heights and oriented in such a way as to respond to the building’s surroundings. The highest box veered toward Mount Rainier, while a lower platform jutted out toward Seattle’s port, suspended in midair, like a dock floating on water. Even the glass-and-steel skin that encased the pile of boxes was tailor-made to the environment, its panels altering in size and density depending on the wind and structural loads of the site.
